content.problemStatement:
Dr.D has recently been thinking about the phrase "Life is short". He decided to start doing all sorts of exotic stuff he has always wanted to do. But since life is short, he does not have the time to do it all by himself, so he made the Cloninator and used it to create NNN clones of himself (numbered 111 through NNN) to help him do all the stuff on his hope-to-do-list. Dr.D has already lined up the clones; for each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th clone in the line is the PiP_iPi​-th clone created by Cloninator.

There are QQQ items on Dr.D's hope-to-do-list, so you should process QQQ queries. For each query, Dr.D wants to choose some parameters LLL and RRR such that 1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N and send the clones PL,PL+1,…,PRP_L, P_{L+1}, \ldots, P_RPL​,PL+1​,…,PR​ to do an exotic activity. Dr.D waits until these clones finish and come back before proceeding with the next item on his list (the next query), so a clone may be picked multiple times in different queries.

The strength of a group of clones is the maximum number of clones in that group such that their positions in the line are consecutive and they can be reordered in such a way that their id-s PiP_iPi​ also become consecutive (they were created consecutively by the Cloninator). Dr.D is wondering if the groups he selects are strong enough for their tasks. For each query, calculate the strength of the group of clones that Dr.D sends to do an exotic activity.
constraints: P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ form a permutation of 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
The next QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY; the values of LLL and RRR for this query can be calculated as follows:

Let lastlastlast be the answer to the previous query, or 000 if this is the first query.
Calculate L=((X+last−1) % N)+1L = ((X + last - 1)\,\%\, N) + 1L=((X+last−1)%N)+1.
Calculate R=((Y+last−1) % N)+1R = ((Y + last - 1)\,\%\, N) + 1R=((Y+last−1)%N)+1.
If L>RL \gt RL>R, swap LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ― the strength of the given group.
